animeVanguardsCodes.title: Anime Vanguards Codes
animeVanguardsCodes.intro: Redeem the latest Anime Vanguards codes for Trait Rerolls, Memoria Shards, Iced Teas, Gems, Gold, Stat Chips, and event materials. Copy active codes, check recently expired codes, and follow the safe redeem path from the Profile menu.
animeVanguardsCodes.lastChecked: Last checked for Eternal Adversaries Update 13.5

activeCodes.rewards: 50 Trait Rerolls, 50 Memoria Shards, 5 Rainbow Essence Stones

redeemSteps.title: Join and Progress
redeemSteps.description: Join Anime Vanguards on Roblox and progress until code redemption is available.

redeemSteps.title: Reach the Level Gate
redeemSteps.description: Reach Level 10 for the basic code menu; newer codes like the Eternal Adversaries set require Level 30.

redeemSteps.title: Open the Profile Menu
redeemSteps.description: Tap the blue Profile button on the right side of the screen.

redeemSteps.title: Select the Codes Option
redeemSteps.description: Choose the purple Codes option inside the Profile window.

redeemSteps.title: Paste and Redeem
redeemSteps.description: Paste the code exactly as written, then press Redeem to receive the rewards.

rewardTypes.name: Trait Rerolls
rewardTypes.description: Reroll unit traits for stronger damage, utility, or support setups.

rewardTypes.name: Memoria Shards
rewardTypes.description: Progress toward Memoria upgrades and event systems.

rewardTypes.name: Iced Teas
rewardTypes.description: Spend on Eternal Adversaries summer event rewards.

rewardTypes.name: Stat Chips
rewardTypes.description: Improve unit stat rolls before committing long-term resources.

rewardTypes.name: Gold
rewardTypes.description: Pay for upgrades, shops, and progression costs.

animeVanguardsBeginnerGuide.title: Anime Vanguards Beginner Guide
animeVanguardsBeginnerGuide.intro: Start with the correct Roblox experience, clear early progression, unlock code redemption, and build a team around summons, upgrades, and events in Anime Vanguards.

steps.title: Join the Correct Game
steps.description: Open Anime Vanguards: Eternal Adversaries on Roblox by Kitawari with game ID 16146832113.

steps.title: Understand the Core Loop
steps.description: Summon units to defend against enemies, level them up, evolve stronger versions, and clear more difficult battles over time.

steps.title: Progress Until Codes Unlock
steps.description: Reach Level 10 before treating code lists as usable. Some newer rewards require higher account levels, including the Level 30 Eternal Adversaries codes.

steps.title: Use Early Rewards on Units First
steps.description: Spend Gems and reroll resources only after you know which unit you are building. Save premium resources for units you plan to use beyond early story clears.

steps.title: Build a Balanced Starter Team
steps.description: Combine damage, crowd control, support, and economy instead of stacking only damage units.

steps.title: Claim Reliable Free Income
steps.description: Daily rewards, achievements, daily quests, weekly quests, and event tasks provide Gems, Trait Rerolls, and progression materials.

steps.title: Move From Story Into Events
steps.description: After your first stable team is built, move into event tasks and rotating content such as Eternal Adversaries rewards, Summer Portals, and Summer event modes.

steps.title: Track Official Updates
steps.description: Use the official changelog, Discord, X account, and YouTube channel before spending rare materials so resource planning follows the current patch.

quickTips: Prioritize one main carry before spreading resources across many units.
Save Trait Rerolls and Stat Chips for units with a clear long-term role.
Clear daily and weekly quests for steady Gem and reroll income.
Check the official changelog before investing in banner pulls.

animeVanguardsUnitTierList.title: Anime Vanguards Unit Tier List
animeVanguardsUnitTierList.intro: A practical tier grid for current Anime Vanguards team building, covering meta cores, strong support pieces, progression units, and new Eternal Adversaries units. Prioritize one main carry, one control or support unit, and one economy or utility slot first.

tiers.description: Top-priority units that anchor late-game teams through damage, crowd control, economy, summons, or sustained pressure.

units.name: Demon Leader (Control)
units.note: Cuts deployment costs and boosts Demon Hunter allies.

units.name: God (Above Heaven)
units.note: Combines large area damage with a freeze-style control window.

units.name: Song Jinwu and Igros
units.note: Summons shadow soldiers without consuming normal placement slots.

units.name: Gujo (Infinity)
units.note: Uses wide-area control to hold enemies while carries deal damage.

units.name: Rogita (Super 4)
units.note: One of the strongest single-target damage picks in the current meta.

tiers.description: High-value units that fit hard content and pair well with SS-tier cores.

units.name: Demon Hybrid (Chainsaw)
units.note: Burst DPS for Demon Hunter teams and bleed detonation.

units.name: Brolzi Super (Wrathful)
units.note: Hard-stage DPS that fits the carry slot.

units.name: Divalo (Requiem)
units.note: Enemy speed control for boss waves and lane safety.

units.name: Teacher (Final Stand)
units.note: Team utility support for hard content.

tiers.description: Reliable units for players building toward harder content without a full SS-tier roster.

units.name: Iscanur (Pride)
units.note: Damage carry for general clears and event stages.

units.name: Okorun (Turbo)
units.note: Fast damage for speed clears and event teams.

units.name: Savior (Moonless)
units.note: Carry for story progression and late-game builds.

units.name: Explosive Demon (Bomb)
units.note: AoE damage for crowded waves and budget teams.

tiers.description: Useful units for players filling a working team before premium Secret, Vanguard, and Exclusive units are ready.

units.name: Deceiver (Monster)
units.note: Fallback carry for progression and story clears.

units.name: Fastest Shinobi (Flash)
units.note: Fast DPS for speed-focused clears and lane control.

units.name: Saber (Black Tyrant)
units.note: Progression carry bridging beginner teams to mid-game.

tiers.description: Newest units from Eternal Adversaries content worth evaluating before spending event materials and reroll resources.

animeVanguardsUnitsGuide.title: Anime Vanguards Units Guide
animeVanguardsUnitsGuide.intro: Learn unit roles, rarities, obtainment paths, starter choices, limited units, and what to check before spending scarce resources in Anime Vanguards.

cards.title: Starter Unit Choice
cards.howToGet: Choose one of the three starter units during early progression.
cards.investmentNote: Use starters to clear the first stages, then shift resources toward stronger summoned, event, Secret, Vanguard, or Exclusive units.

cards.title: Summon Banner Units
cards.units: God (Above Heaven), Gujo (Infinity), Demon Leader (Control), Rogita (Super 4), Ice Queen (Release), Alocard (Vampire King)
cards.howToGet: Use Gems and banner pulls when the target unit is available.
cards.investmentNote: Prioritize units that fill a clear role: carry damage, crowd control, economy support, or boss killing.

cards.title: Eternal Adversaries Banner Target
cards.investmentNote: Treat Vanguard-rarity banner units as high-resource targets and check traits, stats, and Memoria before rerolling heavily.

cards.title: Eternal Adversaries Worldlines Unit
cards.investmentNote: Secret units are long-term candidates when their role fits your main team and current event goals.

cards.title: Eternal Adversaries Event Pass Unit
cards.investmentNote: Event Pass units are valuable for collection and update-specific progression paths.

cards.title: Godly Ascension Battlepass Units
cards.investmentNote: Battlepass units are limited progression targets; check their evolved forms and Memoria links before spending premium rerolls.

cards.title: Godly Ascension Pirate Units
cards.units: Elastic Captain (Cog 5th), Bounty Hunter (King of Hell), Beast Pirate (Half-Dragon), Scrap Pirate (Metal)
cards.howToGet: Evolution Quests, Rift gameplay, and Godly Ascension content
cards.investmentNote: Use Pirate units for Update 13 progression paths such as Pirate Dynasty, Rift rewards, and evolution questlines.

cards.title: Before You Invest
cards.units: Rarity, Obtainment path, Role in team, Trait value, Stat rolls, Evolution materials, Memoria links, Current update usefulness
cards.howToGet: Review this checklist each time you consider spending premium resources.
cards.investmentNote: Spend Trait Rerolls and Stat Chips only after the unit has a clear job in your team.

animeVanguardsTraitsGuide.title: Anime Vanguards Traits and Rerolls Guide
animeVanguardsTraitsGuide.intro: Traits are permanent unit modifiers that improve combat stats such as Damage, SPA, and Range. Learn how Trait Rerolls work, which traits are worth keeping, and where to spend rare reroll resources first in Anime Vanguards.

items.heading: How Trait Rerolls Work
items.content: A Trait Reroll changes the trait on one selected unit. Each attempt consumes one Trait Reroll, and the new result replaces the previous trait on that unit.
keyPoints: Traits can appear when summoning units or be changed later with Trait Rerolls.
Trait Rerolls are spent at the Mandra Traits NPC.
The Trait Index menu tracks mythic pity separately for each unit.
Evolution, Normalizing, and Stat Transfers do not reset a unit's trait pity.

items.heading: Trait Roll Pool
items.content: The current trait pool includes common stat traits, farming traits, and rare mythic traits reserved for endgame units.

traits.name: Vigor
traits.role: Damage boost for early and filler DPS units

traits.name: Range
traits.role: Extra coverage for units that need better placement reach

traits.name: Swift
traits.role: SPA improvement for faster attacking units

traits.name: Marksman
traits.role: Range upgrade for units with strong area coverage

traits.name: Fortune
traits.role: Economy trait for farming and support value

traits.name: Blitz
traits.role: High-value SPA trait for damage units

traits.name: Solar
traits.role: Mythic trait with strong damage and range value

traits.name: Deadeye
traits.role: Mythic trait for crit-focused boss and infinite damage

traits.name: Ethereal
traits.role: Top mythic damage trait for major carry units

traits.name: Monarch
traits.role: Highest-value mythic trait for premium carry units

items.heading: Mythic Pity Targets
items.content: Mythic traits have pity thresholds that help you plan reroll sessions instead of clicking until resources run out.

items.heading: Best Reroll Priority
items.content: Rerolls should go to units that remain useful after evolution, ascension planning, and current endgame farming.
priorityOrder: Main evolved DPS used across Story, Legend, Raid, Rift, Odyssey, and Boss Rush teams
Vanguard, Secret, and high-value Mythic units with strong long-term kits
Boss killers with high damage scaling, crit value, or powerful active abilities
Core support units only when their trait directly improves their role
Temporary leveling units only after the main team already has usable traits

items.heading: Role-Based Trait Matching
items.content: The best trait depends on what the unit actually does inside a team.

roleMatches.reason: These units benefit heavily from large stat gains because every placement matters.
SPA and crit-focused traits improve repeated damage output.
Range and damage together improve map coverage and wave clearing.
Economy units care more about income timing and utility than raw damage.
Common traits are acceptable until the unit is replaced.

animeVanguardsEvolutionsGuide.title: Anime Vanguards Evolution and Ascension Guide
animeVanguardsEvolutionsGuide.intro: Evolution changes a unit into a stronger form with improved stats, attacks, passives, or upgrade paths in Anime Vanguards. Ascension is a separate long-term upgrade for Mythic units, so the safest route is to evolve core units first, then ascend only the units that will stay in your main teams.

steps.title: Choose the Final Unit Before Farming
steps.description: Start with units that already carry your Story, Legend, Raid, Rift, Odyssey, Worldlines, or Boss Rush teams.
checklist: Check whether the unit has an evolved form.
Check whether the evolved form changes its attack pattern, passive, or upgrade value.
Prioritize units that stay useful across several modes.

steps.title: Farm the Required Evolution Route
steps.description: Evolution requirements vary by unit. Some units need specific evolution items, while others also require Evolution Quests.
checklist: Farm the stage, raid, rift, event shop, or mode tied to the required item.
Save essence stones, gold, and unit-specific materials until the full requirement is ready.
Avoid partial investment into units that are not part of the final team.

steps.title: Complete Evolution Quests When Required
steps.description: Some major units use Evolution Quests as part of their upgrade path. These quests are multi-step tasks that must be finished before the evolution can be completed.

steps.title: Evolve the Unit at the Upgrade Area
steps.description: Use the Evolve station in the Upgrades area once the unit, materials, gold, and quest requirements are ready.
steps.result: A successful evolution can add stronger stats, new attacks, new passives, more upgrade levels, or a stronger final role.

steps.title: Use Current Memoria Rules
steps.description: Update 13.5 changed Memoria progression. Memoria evolutions were removed, all Memoria are evolved by default, and unit evolutions now grant their designated Memoria.
resourceNotes: Memoria Recycling turns unwanted Memoria into rarity-based Memoria Scrap.
Memoria Leveling Tickets can instantly raise Memoria to Level 60.
Memoria stat transfer support helps protect investment when upgrading builds.

steps.title: Ascend Only Long-Term Mythic Units
steps.description: Ascension is handled through the Kokashi NPC and applies only to Mythic units. It gives three stat upgrades across three ascension levels.

steps.title: Prepare Duplicate and Material Costs
steps.description: A full ascension plan requires the target unit, spare non-evolved unlocked non-shiny copies, and unit-specific materials.
steps.safetyRule: Do not spend duplicate copies on a unit unless that unit is staying in your account plan.

steps.title: Protect Ascension Before Rarity-Changing Evolution
steps.description: When a Mythic unit evolves into Secret or Vanguard rarity, its ascensions are removed and do not transfer to the new form.
steps.bestUse: Finish rarity-changing evolution decisions first, then use ascension resources on the unit form that will remain in your team.

animeVanguardsGameModesGuide.title: Anime Vanguards Game Modes Guide
animeVanguardsGameModesGuide.intro: Anime Vanguards is built around repeated tower-defense progression, but each mode pushes a different goal: story unlocks, rare drops, event currency, evolution items, rerolls, Memoria resources, or boss-focused farming. Use this overview to pick the right mode for your current goal.

modes.name: Story Mode
modes.description: Clear acts across themed worlds on Normal and Nightmare modes.
modes.teamFocus: Cheap early AoE, one reliable boss DPS, one farm or support unit, and stable upgrades.
modes.keyRewards: Gems, Gold, player progression, stage drops, and access to later content.
modes.farmWhen: Use this first to unlock worlds, prepare materials, and build the account foundation.

modes.name: Legend Stages
modes.description: Clear stronger versions of story maps with modifiers and exclusive reward paths.
modes.teamFocus: High damage carry, strong AoE coverage, slows or control, and upgraded units.
modes.keyRewards: Evolution materials, rare stage drops, unit drop pity progress, and account progression.
modes.farmWhen: Farm after the core team can clear Story consistently.

modes.name: Raids
modes.description: Fight harder multi-wave stages and boss encounters for exclusive rewards.
modes.teamFocus: Boss DPS, wave clear, support, slows, and coordinated co-op placement.
modes.keyRewards: Rare units, evolution items, event currency, and raid-exclusive rewards.
modes.farmWhen: Farm when the team has evolved carries and enough support to survive boss pressure.

modes.name: Rifts
modes.description: Enter rift encounters with dangerous enemies and limited reward windows.
modes.teamFocus: Fast clear speed, strong boss damage, and units that can handle sudden pressure.
modes.keyRewards: Rare rewards, rift units, event items, and update-based drops.
modes.farmWhen: Farm when a current update places key units or materials behind rift gameplay.

modes.name: Boss Rush and Boss Events
modes.description: Fight boss-heavy waves and rotating boss encounters for event and progression rewards.
modes.teamFocus: Single-target damage, status support, burst abilities, and reliable late-wave scaling.
modes.keyRewards: Boss rewards, event currency, unit drops, and progression resources.
modes.farmWhen: Farm when the account needs boss-exclusive drops or event shop currency.

modes.name: Odyssey
modes.description: Progress through Odyssey runs using mode-specific powers, cards, characters, and scaling choices.
modes.teamFocus: Durable carry units, long-run scaling, resource-efficient upgrades, and flexible support.
modes.keyRewards: Trait Rerolls, Memoria Shards, mode resources, and update-based Odyssey rewards.
modes.farmWhen: Farm when the account needs rerolls, Memoria resources, or repeatable endgame progression.

modes.name: Worldlines
modes.purpose: Floor-based progression and seasonal reward farming
modes.description: Clear floors with increasing difficulty, unique map challenges, and seasonal reward resets.
modes.teamFocus: Consistent wave control, boss damage, and units that handle scaling enemies.
modes.keyRewards: Floor rewards, Trait Rerolls, Memoria Shards, Stat Chips, essence stones, and seasonal units.
modes.farmWhen: Farm each reset cycle to claim floor milestones and new seasonal rewards.

modes.name: Summer Gamemode
modes.description: Fight endless zombie-style waves with a Summer theme, unique powers, and mode-specific unit choices.
modes.teamFocus: Endurance, wave control, fast upgrades, and units that scale during long survival runs.
modes.keyRewards: Iced Tea, Summer event items, Summer rewards, and Event Pass progress.
modes.farmWhen: Farm during Update 13.5 while Eternal Adversaries and Iced Tea Festival rewards are active.

modes.name: Summer Portals
modes.description: Clear Summer Portal content for exclusive portal rewards.
modes.teamFocus: Strong burst damage, broad support coverage, and steady wave clears against portal modifiers.
modes.keyRewards: Vanguard Familiars, Summer rewards, and exclusive portal prizes.
modes.farmWhen: Farm when building Summer units or chasing new Vanguard Familiar upgrades.

animeVanguardsUpdates.title: Anime Vanguards Updates and Events Tracker
animeVanguardsUpdates.intro: Anime Vanguards updates usually add new units, event modes, currencies, banners, evolution routes, Memoria changes, and limited-time reward tracks. This tracker organizes the most important changes by update so returning players can jump into the right farming route quickly.

entries.title: Eternal Adversaries (Update 13.5)
highlights: Social Hub, 3 unit remodels, 4 new cosmetics, 34 quality-of-life changes, and 356 bug fixes
entries.playerFocus: Farm Iced Tea, progress the Event Pass, test Summer Portals for Vanguard Familiar rewards, recycle duplicate Memoria, and review unit builds after the Memoria system changes.

entries.title: Godly Ascension (Update 13.0)
highlights: Odyssey changes and new special events
entries.playerFocus: Work on the new evolution routes, clear Pirate Dynasty rewards, challenge Boss Rush, and farm the new Rift path for update-specific drops.

entries.title: Turning Tides (Update 12.5)
entries.playerFocus: Use the Odyssey rework for repeatable resource farming, rebuild Elemental Tower progress, and check guild features before focusing only on summons.

entries.title: Demonic Rampage (Update 11.0)
highlights: New Unit Memoria and Standard Memoria Banner
entries.playerFocus: Clear Hill of Swords progression, learn Dispersal enemy behavior, and target Memoria upgrades for units that returned to stronger use.

entries.title: Frozen Solitude (Update 10.5)
highlights: Winter Shop and Legacy Event Shop
Worldlines reset and new limited events
entries.playerFocus: Use seasonal shops for evolution items, claim Worldlines floor rewards, and collect Winter Banner units or Memoria before rotating content changes.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Anime Vanguards

questions.question: What is Anime Vanguards?
questions.answer: Anime Vanguards is a Roblox anime tower defense game by Kitawari where players summon units, level and evolve them, and clear story stages, raids, and limited-time events.

questions.question: How do I redeem Anime Vanguards codes?
questions.answer: Reach Level 10, open the blue Profile button, select the purple Codes option, paste the code, and press Redeem. Newer Eternal Adversaries codes require Level 30.

questions.question: Where can I play Anime Vanguards?
questions.answer: Anime Vanguards is playable on Roblox on PC and mobile. Join the official experience by Kitawari with game ID 16146832113.

questions.question: How do I get stronger units in Anime Vanguards?
questions.answer: Use Gems for banner summons, then upgrade, reroll traits, improve stats, and evolve units that hold a clear role in your team.

questions.question: What are the best resources to save in Anime Vanguards?
questions.answer: Save Trait Rerolls and Stat Chips for Secret, Vanguard, and Exclusive units. Spend Gems on banners that fit your team goals.

questions.question: Where can I follow Anime Vanguards updates?
questions.answer: Follow the official Anime Vanguards Discord, YouTube channel, X account, and the vanguards.gg changelog for the latest patch notes, new units, and event details.
